title: WordPress REST Block Renderer Flow

steps: Get all registered block types from WP_Block_Type_Registry::get_instance()->get_all_registered()
For each block_type: check if block is dynamic (has render_callback)
Route args: name (string), context (default='view'), attributes (object), post_id (integer)
[post not found OR user cannot 'edit_post'] → return WP_Error('block_cannot_read', 401/403)
[post found AND user can edit_post] → continue
[post_id > 0] → call get_post(post_id), setup_postdata($post) — sets global $post context
[block NOT registered / null] → return WP_Error('block_invalid', 'Invalid block.', 404)
Construct block array: {blockName, attrs, innerHTML:'', innerContent:[]}
Call render_block($block) — invokes all registered filters + block's render_callback
[schema already cached ($this->schema is set)] → return $this->schema immediately
properties: { rendered: { type: 'string', required: true, context: ['edit'] } }

conditions_and_branches: is_dynamic() check → only dynamic blocks get REST routes registered
post_id > 0 → post-context permission path (requires edit_post on specific post)
post_id == 0 → general permission path (requires edit_posts capability)
block registered? → null check determines 404 vs render
schema cached? → short-circuit return vs full schema build
key_relationships: WP_REST_Block_Renderer_Controller depends_on WP_Block_Type_Registry (get all blocks, get single block)
WP_REST_Block_Renderer_Controller calls render_block() WordPress core function
WP_REST_Block_Renderer_Controller calls get_post(), setup_postdata(), current_user_can() WordPress core
error_states: WP_Error('block_cannot_read') — user lacks edit_post on specified post (post_id > 0)
WP_Error('block_invalid', 404) — requested block name not registered in registry
important_notes: Controller registered at wp/v2/block-renderer/{block-name}. Only dynamic blocks (those with a render_callback) get routes. The rendered output is wrapped in {rendered: html} and context is restricted to 'edit' only. Schema is cached after first build. Post context (global $post) is set up when post_id is provided to support blocks relying on get_the_ID() etc. render_block() ensures all block_render filters fire.
